cards.title: Definition & Determinants
cards.content:
ΓÇó WHO Definition: A state of complete physical, mental, and social well-being, not just absence of disease.
ΓÇó Determinants of Health: Lifestyle, Genetics, Environment, and Medical Services.

cards.title: Levels of Healthcare
cards.content:
ΓÇó Primary: First point of contact (e.g., Clinics, Primary Health Centers).
ΓÇó Secondary: Specialized hospitals for specific ailments (General Hospitals).
ΓÇó Tertiary: Highly specialized, advanced care (e.g., Cancer Centers, Cardiology units).

items.text: ESIS (Employees State Insurance Scheme): Introduced for factory workers and their families.
CGHS (Central Govt. Health Scheme): Introduced for Central Government employees.
Mediclaim: The first commercial health insurance policy introduced in India.

sections.title: ≡ƒÆí Exam Master Tips
items: Types of Healthcare: Preventive (vaccines), Promotive (health education), Curative (treatment), Rehabilitative (restoring normal life).
SAHI: Stand-alone Health Insurance companies specialize ONLY in health, travel, and personal accident insurance.
TPA (Third Party Administrator): Intermediary who manages claims, hospital networks, and cashless processing on behalf of insurers.
Lifestyle diseases (like Diabetes and Hypertension) are currently the biggest burden on the Indian healthcare system.
Health insurance mitigates the financial impact of out-of-pocket medical expenses.
